ulf_hook: The dead do not rest in the meadows. The warrior takes their bones to mend his own. A beast of the stars is led into a cage. The earth bleeds fire in the dark halls. The Elder’s name is written in stone. The forest hunts at night.
grandpa_legend: Well, it was a busy day for our young exile, and I had to start by fixing up that workbench roof so the rain wouldn't keep us from our work. We managed to bring home a queen bee with a lucky bow shot and set up a few more beehives to keep the honey flowing. The real trick was leading those boars—one of 'em had the strength of stars—into the pen, where we're taming them with some fresh mushrooms and berries. I felt a bit of a hitch-in-my-gitty-up when that Troll chased us out of the Black Forest, but we made it back with five Surtling Cores and the location of The Elder. It’s a bitter lesson to learn that the woods get mean after dark, but with our new leather gear and a level three workbench, we're finally starting to feel at home.
conrad_chronicle: The air in the meadows grew cold as I struck down a rattling shadow, its bone fragments heavy in my hands. I found the Golden Ones humming in the eaves and brought their queen to our Othala to provide the sweetness we need. Leading the star-touched tusk-beasts into their enclosure was a test of patience, their grunts softening as they tasted the sower's tithe of red berries. Deep within the earth, the Burial Chamber smelled of ancient dust, but the five glowing hearts I pulled from the dark will soon feed the forge. I touched the cold stone of the Vegvisir and felt the weight of the Forsaken king, The Elder, pressing against my mind. The night in the forest was a blur of heavy footfalls and the stench of a shadow in the trees, my lungs heaving as I fled toward the safety of the hearth. Now, wrapped in my new deer hide cape, the leather mended and strong, I listen to the wind and prepare for the trial ahead.
